title: OKLM Conversion Report
description: JSON Schema for the machine-readable report every OKLM conversion or export must produce (see CONVERSIONS.md), draft 0.2. Core principle: never silently discard information — losses are explicit. Covers the two mandatory bidirectional directions oklm-to-ldml and ldml-to-oklm, plus the one-way v1 export directions oklm-to-xkb and oklm-to-keylayout (see CONVERSIONS.md "Other export targets"); import from xkb/keylayout remains out of scope.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the conversion report schema.

compatibilityLevel.description: lossless-core: core mapping preserved. lossy-metadata: mapping preserved, metadata lost. lossy-mapping: mapping approximated, manual review required. failed: conversion not usable.

mappedFields.description: Source fields successfully converted, as dotted paths (e.g. "keys", "deadKeys", "metadata.description").

skippedFields.description: Source fields not converted at all, each with an explicit reason. For oklm-to-ldml this is where OKLM-only metadata losses are declared.

lossyMappings.description: Fields converted with approximation or partial loss of semantics.

preservedAsExtensions.description: ldml-to-oklm only: LDML constructs not mapped to core OKLM but preserved verbatim in an extensions namespace.

unsupportedConstructs.description: ldml-to-oklm only: LDML constructs that could be neither mapped nor preserved.

suggestedEnrichmentTasks.description: ldml-to-oklm only: suggested OKLM metadata to add after import (legends, training, accessibility...).

roundTripConfidence.description: Expected fidelity of converting the result back to the source format.

endpoint.description: One side of the conversion. For OKLM files, version is the OKLM schemaVersion; for LDML files, version is the CLDR/UTS #35 version and conformsTo the declared keyboard3 conformance number (whole number >= 45); xkb-symbols and apple-keylayout files do not use version/conformsTo.

file.description: File name or path, informative only.

fieldPath.description: Dotted path into the source document, e.g. "deadKeys", "metadata.training".
